title: Gibbs free energy change, ΔG
summary: 9701 A Level thermodynamics — ΔG = ΔH − TΔS, spontaneity, and links to equilibrium and electrochemistry.
learningObjectives: Calculate ΔG from ΔH and ΔS at a given temperature.
Predict feasibility of reactions using ΔG sign.
Relate ΔG° to equilibrium constant K.

simpleExplanation.title: Will it happen?
simpleExplanation.summary: ΔG combines enthalpy and entropy effects. Negative ΔG means spontaneous under stated conditions; at equilibrium ΔG = 0.
steps: ΔG = ΔH − TΔS — units: kJ mol⁻¹ if ΔS in J K⁻¹ mol⁻¹ (watch ÷1000).
ΔG < 0: feasible; ΔG > 0: not feasible (reverse may be).
ΔG° = −RT ln K — large K when ΔG° very negative.
Temperature can flip feasibility if ΔH and TΔS oppose.

sections.content: **Gibbs free energy** (23.4) ties **entropy** (23.3), **equilibrium** (7.1), and **electrochemistry** (24.2). Show **unit conversions** for ΔS explicitly.

sections.label: Mark a Gibbs free energy question
